rules.description: For nonstandard chess boards, replace fixed center and edge assumptions with graph centrality, shortest-path mobility, king escape regions, and topology-aware attack maps.
Do not apply standard PGN openings directly on torus, cylinder, polar, Mobius, RP2, 3D, or 4D boards; transfer only activity, safety, material, and graph-distance features.

For topology chess, build attack and defense maps on the actual graph. Protected valuable pieces, safe trades, and king escape regions transfer better than ordinary standard-board opening memorization.

For 3D chess, opening play should occupy high-centrality volume lanes, activate long-range pieces on straight graph rays, and keep the king with multiple escape layers instead of copying ordinary 2D opening names.
On wrapped or twisted chess boards, search seam tactics explicitly: a line that crosses a seam can attack the king or hang a valuable piece even when it appears distant in the rendered view.
